system.description: You gain the ability to create Minor Anomalies (ask the GM to share the page with you). Place any coin or bill on the ground and roll [quality].

outcomes.success:
the money turns into a Minor Anomaly of any type. It follows your instructions intelligently, and won’t act against you. If you die or use this Ability while a previous instance is still active, you lose control of the Minor Anomaly. What it does with its freedom depends on how it has been treated, but typically it will leave you behind to find another Anomaly to attach to.

These Minor Anomalies must share some cosmetic resemblance to you or your personal Anomaly. Disruptive Minor Anomalies only affect rolls you make, and they add one 3 to your rolls rather than one Chaos. These 3s are added after Triscendence is determined.
outcomes.sixplus3s: you may add an additional category to the Minor Anomaly, combining its Stability, Harm, and skills into one form. You cannot select the same category twice.
outcomes.triscendence: you may use the Triple Chaos Effect instead, creating three Minor Anomalies. They follow all the same rules.
outcomes.failure: another Anomaly seizes control over your process. Three hostile Minor Anomalies are immediately created.

improvement.question: Which do I use most to reach my goals?
